meta.description: AI-driven ad-campaign automation SaaS. Scrapes a business's website, runs an AI research pipeline (product/audience/competitor/market/persona), generates strategy + creative (copy, AI image, optional AI video), and launches/optimizes real Meta and Google ad campaigns. Includes an AI 'Strategist' chat and a separate 'Copilot' chat.
meta.architecture_style: hybrid monolith + partially-coupled microservices. NOT a clean microservice fleet: auth-service and campaign-service are separate OS processes but import apps/api's TypeScript source directly via relative paths (../../api/src/modules/...), so they cannot be built or deployed independently of that source tree. scraper-service is genuinely independent (own pipeline, own Prisma import). 5 additional standalone Node processes are BullMQ workers. One Python FastAPI sidecar exists as an AI-research fallback.
meta.repo_layout: npm workspaces monorepo: apps/web, apps/api, apps/auth-service, apps/campaign-service, apps/scraper-service (+ apps/scraper-service/python), docker-compose.yml (Postgres+Redis only, NO app containers), .github/workflows/ci.yml (typecheck+build only), docs/architecture-roadmap.md (STALE target-state doc — claims no Postgres/Redis/queues/service-extraction/CI, all 4 already exist)
meta.generated_from: direct source inspection via parallel research agents across the full repo, not inferred

services.name: web
services.runtime: Vite dev server (5173) / static build in prod
services.state_management: React Context only (AuthContext, CopilotProvider) + local component useState — no Redux/Zustand/MobX, no React Query/SWR
services.api_client: single hand-written fetch wrapper at src/api/client.ts, ~90 one-line domain methods, attaches Authorization: Bearer <token> only if a token exists, throws normalized Error on non-OK (extractErrorMessage strips internal-looking messages before reaching UI)
services.notes: never calls auth-service/campaign-service/scraper-service directly — always through apps/api gateway. No component library (hand-rolled CSS in one large styles.css).

services.name: api
responsibilities: HTTP ingress for the entire product (~50+ routes in gateway/router.ts)
auth middleware (requireAuth JWT check, dev-mode bypass when NODE_ENV != production)
reverse-proxy to auth-service/campaign-service/scraper-service via proxyTo() (NO timeout, NO retry — flagged gap)
in-process business logic for most non-proxied resource areas (~25 modules under src/modules/*)
hosts the OpenAI client wrapper, encryption utilities, event bus, object storage, vector store placeholders
middleware_order: express.json({verify: capture rawBody for Meta webhook HMAC})
route-specific: authEntryRouter (unauthenticated) then requireAuth-gated router, or crmInternalAuth for /api/crm/*, or unauthenticated for OAuth redirect + Meta webhook routes

services.name: auth-service
services.coupling: imports apps/api/src/modules/auth/* and apps/api/src/modules/workspace/* directly via relative path — NOT an HTTP call, NOT independently deployable from apps/api's source
own_files: src/internalAuth.ts (byte-for-byte duplicate of campaign-service's and scraper-service's copy)
src/loadEnv.ts (loads apps/api/.env, not its own — 'shares apps/api's .env rather than duplicating secrets')
services.auth_stack_per_route: internalServiceAuth (shared x-internal-service-key header check, fails closed/500 if env var unset) -> requireUser (JWT signature+expiry) -> per-route membership/role check
responsibilities: workspace-membership CRUD with owner/admin role checks

services.name: campaign-service
services.coupling: imports apps/api/src/modules/{orchestrator,pipeline,optimization,insights,billing,strategy,onboarding/researchSessionService}/* directly via relative path — same non-independent-deployability caveat as auth-service
services.auth_stack_per_route: internalServiceAuth only — NO per-user JWT check at this service; authorization for these routes relies entirely on the gateway having already checked requireAuth before proxying
responsibilities: campaign build (from strategy or from AI research suggestions)

services.name: scraper-service
services.coupling: genuinely independent — has its own Prisma import and own service-local pipeline modules; only shares types/conventions with apps/api
services.browser_management: single shared chromium.launch({headless:true}) instance reused process-wide; each request gets its own isolated browser.newContext(); closeBrowser() called on SIGTERM
talks_to: openai (LLM normalize + creative gen + campaign suggestion stages)

services.name: python-research-sidecar
services.role: ONLY used as the deep-research pipeline's no-OPENAI_API_KEY fallback — never used by the onboarding cheerio scraper or the Playwright product-import pipeline
services.important_behavior: receives the ALREADY-SCRAPED site excerpt from apps/api rather than re-fetching the URL itself, because scrapegraphai's own headless-browser re-fetch has been observed to hang indefinitely on JS-heavy sites (e.g. stripe.com) — explicit code comment
services.quality_note: produces structurally valid JSON but often with placeholder junk ('na'/'unknown'/'tbd') often enough that callers run a blockUsable() heuristic (reject if >=50% of fields look like junk) before falling through to a static fallback

workers.name: creativeGenerationWorker
processing_steps: load GenerationJob row by jobId
markGenerationJobRunning
resolve context: explicit prompt, OR scrape productUrl via scrapeUrl() (onboarding pipeline) with failure falling back to raw URL
generateCreativeBrief() — OpenAI runStructured tool-call -> {headline, body, callToAction, imagePrompt}, or static fallbackBrief if no OPENAI_API_KEY
getImageProvider().generate(brief.imagePrompt) — OpenAI gpt-image-1 via raw fetch, or MockImageProvider (1x1 PNG) if no key
upload image to objectStorage (local disk), createAsset
IF wantVideo: getVideoProvider().generateFromImage() — Runway gen4_turbo, or MockVideoProvider (empty buffer) if no RUNWAY_API_KEY; upload + createAsset
createCreative
markGenerationJobDone
workers.failure_handling: whole body wrapped in try/catch inside runGenerationJob itself; on error calls markGenerationJobFailed then RE-THROWS so BullMQ's attempts:2/5s-exponential retry is real. worker.on('failed') only logs.

workers.name: leadIngestionWorker

job_data_shapes.ingest-one: { workspaceId, leadgenId } — enqueued by the Meta webhook handler for fast ack
job_data_shapes.backfill: { workspaceId, platform } — manual 'Sync Recent Leads' trigger

processing_steps: ingest-one -> ingestMetaLead() in modules/leadgen/metaLeadSync.ts (fetch one lead's field_data from Meta Graph v22.0, upsert via ingestLead())
backfill + platform=meta -> backfillMetaLeads() (list all lead-gen forms on the connected Page, paginate every form's leads via Graph API cursor, upsert each)
backfill + platform=google -> syncGoogleLeadForms() then syncGoogleLeadSubmissions()
either branch finishes with updateIntegrationSettings(..., {lastLeadSyncAt})
unknown job name -> logger.warn and swallowed (job 'succeeds' with no work done)
workers.failure_handling: no try/catch in the worker function itself — any thrown error (e.g. graphGet's explicit throw on Graph API error) bubbles to BullMQ, driving attempts:3/5s-exponential retry.

workers.name: researchSessionWorker
processing_steps: markResearchSessionRunning
scrapeUrl(url) — reuses the onboarding cheerio pipeline

sequential AI blocks, each preceded by setResearchSessionCurrentStep and followed by appendResearchBlock: analyzeProductDeep -> analyzeAudienceDeep -> analyzeCompetitorsAndBudget -> analyzeMarketAndLocation -> mineAudiencePersonas (this last block NEVER uses live web search)
setResearchSessionPersonas
markResearchSessionDone
workers.search_budget: session-local counter gated by MAX_SEARCHES_PER_SESSION=16 (defined in modules/onboarding/researchSessionService.ts); once exhausted, later blocks run with allowSearch=false (reasoning-only, no live search)
workers.failure_handling: whole body in try/catch calling markResearchSessionFailed(sessionId, message) then RE-THROWS. Queue has attempts:1 (no retry) BY DESIGN — a partially-completed run has already spent billed web searches, so silent retry would double-spend.

workers.name: metricsIngestionWorker
workers.repeatable_schedule: self-registers at module top-level (top-level await) on worker startup: metricsIngestionQueue.add(REPEATABLE_JOB_NAME, {}, {repeat: {every: 15*60*1000}, jobId: REPEATABLE_JOB_NAME}) — plain millisecond interval, NOT a cron expression; fixed jobId makes re-registration idempotent across worker restarts (BullMQ replaces rather than stacks the schedule)
processing_steps: listActiveCampaigns() — Postgres JSON-path filter (data.status == 'active') across ALL businesses/workspaces
for each campaign, SEQUENTIALLY (not parallel): ingestCampaignMetrics(campaignId) -> runOptimizationPass(campaignId) -> recordOptimizationInsights(workspaceId, decisions)
workers.failure_handling: PER-CAMPAIGN try/catch inside the loop — one campaign's failure is logged and the loop continues; BullMQ's attempts:2 essentially never engages for per-campaign errors since they're swallowed after one attempt with only a log line.
workers.fragility_flag: concurrency 1 + single global repeatable job + fully sequential per-campaign loop means a tick that runs longer than 15 minutes (plausible as active-campaign count grows) causes the next tick to queue up behind it — self-limiting but with no alerting on tick duration or backlog.

workers.name: crmWebhookWorker
job_names: (job name = the CRM event string itself, e.g. 'lead.created')
processing_steps: re-fetch getCrmWebhookConfig(workspaceId) AT DELIVERY TIME (not enqueue time) so secret rotation/URL clearing takes effect on retries
no-op silently if config is now null
build envelope { event, version: 'v1', workspace_id, sent_at, data }
HMAC-SHA256 sign body with workspace secret if configured -> header x-polluxa-signature-256: sha256=... (NO signature header at all if no secret configured)
fetch(config.url, {method: POST, ..., signal: AbortSignal.timeout(5000)})
workers.failure_handling: non-2xx response logged and manually thrown; network errors/timeouts propagate untouched — both drive BullMQ retry via custom backoffStrategy
workers.custom_backoff_note: backoffStrategy: (attemptsMade) => CRM_WEBHOOK_BACKOFF_DELAYS_MS[attemptsMade-1] ?? -1 — array bounds line up exactly with attempts:5 so the ?? -1 fallback never actually triggers
workers.dead_letter_behavior: after 5th failed attempt, job sits in BullMQ's Redis 'failed' ZSET for 30 days (removeOnFail age) as the DE FACTO dead-letter store — NO separate delivery-log UI/table exists; only manual Redis inspection surfaces these

queues.name: research-session
queues.backoff: none (deliberate — no retry, cost control against double-spending billed web searches)

queues.name: metrics-ingestion
producers: self-registered repeatable job, 15-minute interval, registered by metricsIngestionWorker on its own startup

queues.name: crm-webhook
producers: crmWebhookService.dispatchCrmWebhook() — called by leadIngestionService.ingestLead() on genuinely-new leads only ('lead.created' event)
queues.dead_letter: the 30-day-retained 'failed' set IS the dead-letter store; no dedicated DLQ table/UI

modules.name: business
issues: NO workspace-scoping on any function — getBusiness/updateBusiness take bare id, listBusinesses returns every business in the DB with no filter

modules.name: strategy
notes: createStrategyFromResearch/FromSuggestions deliberately make NO new OpenAI call — reuse already-gathered research JSON
truncateHeadline/sanitizeCreatives enforce a uniform 40-char headline cap across all 3 creative-generation paths
generateCampaignSuggestions forces exactly 6 Meta + 6 Google suggestions (TikTok excluded, 'Coming soon')

modules.name: analytics
issues: week/month period re-derives spend/impressions/clicks/conversions from raw per-day metrics per campaign — O(campaigns x days), a naive re-implementation of what normalizePerformance already computes for the 'all' case

modules.name: orchestrator.campaignOrchestrator
notes: THE most central module in the app
launchMetaHierarchy/launchGoogleHierarchy build real Campaign->AdSet->Creative->Ad object graphs, everything lands PAUSED
launchCampaign default workspaceId='demo' if caller forgets to pass one
listActiveCampaigns is an all-workspace Postgres JSON-path scan powering metricsIngestionWorker
publishes 'campaign.launched' on the in-process InMemoryEventBus after a successful launch
NO rollback/compensation if a multi-step ad-platform build partially fails (e.g. AdSet succeeds but Ad upload fails — AdSet is never cleaned up on-platform)

modules.name: orchestrator.creativesService
issues: getCreative/deleteCreative take bare id, no workspace/business check

modules.name: insights
notes: dual-source design: 'ai_generated' rows wiped+replaced wholesale each generateInsights call; 'optimizer' rows are a permanent never-auto-cleared action log written by recordOptimizationInsights

modules.name: notifications.notificationService
issues: markAllRead: fetches every workspace notification into JS then does ONE UPSERT PER UNREAD ROW in a loop — no batched UPDATE, documented SQLite-legacy workaround never migrated
unreadCount loads all (capped 50) and filters in JS instead of a COUNT query

modules.name: audience.savedAudienceService
notes: normalizeAudience() backfills type/platform/lookalikeSourceId defaults on read for legacy rows
issues: getSavedAudience/updateSavedAudience/deleteSavedAudience take bare id, no workspace check

modules.name: crm.crmWebhookService
prisma_models: Workspace (crmWebhookUrl/crmWebhookSecret fields inline, no dedicated table)
notes: dispatchCrmWebhook only ENQUEUES — the actual HTTP call happens in crmWebhookWorker.ts
no-op silently if no crmWebhookUrl configured

modules.name: adInsights.adInsightsService
notes: pure derived-data module, no direct Prisma access
buildRealInsights aggregates topAudiences/topPages/scatter/topAds all in-memory via Maps
buildDemoInsights returns fully hardcoded demo data when a business has zero real performance rows for that network

modules.name: integrations.integrationService
notes: sanitizeIntegration strips 5 known encrypted-token keys before any Integration is sent to the browser — required for every route returning one
no compound unique constraint on (workspaceId, platform) at the DB level — relies on application logic (findFirst before create) never creating duplicates

modules.name: integrations.metaOAuth
notes: mock-connects immediately (no live App credentials) rather than redirecting to a broken OAuth dialog
multi-account picker explicitly deferred — only first ad account + first Page used

modules.name: integrations.googleOAuth
exports: hasLiveGoogleAppCredentials (requires client id AND secret AND developer token)
notes: only integration with real token-refresh lifecycle handling in the whole app
exchangeCodeForTokens throws an actionable error if Google doesn't return a refresh_token (stale-consent case)

modules.name: integrations.oauthState
notes: JWT-signed 10-minute-lived {workspaceId} state param, shared by every ad-platform OAuth flow
falls back to hardcoded 'dev-secret-change-me' if JWT_SECRET unset — real secret-management gap

modules.name: integrations.productCatalogService
notes: facebook/google catalog sources are demo-only, no live adapter ('Phase 5')
falls back to hardcoded MOCK_CATALOGS on adapter absence or fetch failure

modules.name: adapters.metaTargetingMapper
notes: resolveAudienceTargetingForWorkspace: case-insensitive name match against SavedAudience, falls back to BROAD_DEFAULT_TARGETING (18-65, US) on no match — the audience-name string-matching bridge, a known fragile seam

modules.name: adapters.googleTargetingMapper
notes: interests become KEYWORDS here (Search campaigns target keywords not interest categories, unlike Meta)
STATIC_GEO_TARGET_IDS: hardcoded table of 13 countries as fallback when live geoTargetConstants:suggest call unavailable/fails

modules.name: adapters.AdAdapter
notes: tiktokAdapter implements only the flat AdAdapter interface, NOT HierarchyCapableAdapter — comment: 'stays on the flat launchVariant path until it gets the same depth in a follow-up'

modules.name: pipeline.performancePipeline
notes: ESTIMATED_REVENUE_CENTS_PER_CONVERSION is the single shared assumed-AOV constant powering ROAS everywhere in the app — no real pixel-fired purchase-value tracking exists anywhere
reaches directly into campaignOrchestrator's exported adapters map rather than any adapter-registry abstraction

modules.name: generation.generationJobService
issues: getGenerationJob/mark-* take bare id, no workspace check

modules.name: generation.imageProvider
notes: raw fetch to https://api.openai.com/v1/images/generations, model gpt-image-1 — bypasses openaiClient.ts's SDK helpers entirely, separate REST surface, NO retry logic

modules.name: generation.videoProvider
notes: Runway (NOT OpenAI), gen4_turbo, image-to-video only
base https://api.dev.runwayml.com/v1, header X-Runway-Version: 2024-11-06
poll GET /tasks/{id} every 4s up to 45 attempts (~3min)
NO retry on any of its 3 fetch calls (create/poll/download)

modules.name: leadgen.leadIngestionService
notes: ingestLead only triggers contactService.upsertContactForLead + crmWebhookService.dispatchCrmWebhook on a GENUINELY NEW lead (checked via existingLead lookup before upsert) — redelivery/backfill re-runs don't double-fire either side effect
properly workspaceId-scoped, unlike several sibling modules

modules.name: leadgen.contactService
notes: matches by email first, phone as fallback ('stronger identity signal')

IMPORTANT CALLER CONTRACT: must be called only once per genuinely-new Lead — leadCount incremented unconditionally with NO idempotency check inside this function itself; idempotency is entirely leadIngestionService's responsibility

modules.name: leadgen.metaLeadSync
notes: resolveWorkspaceIdForMetaPage LINEAR-SCANS ALL meta-platform Integration rows on EVERY inbound webhook delivery — O(n) full-table scan, a real scalability concern at higher workspace counts

modules.name: leadgen.googleLeadSyncService
notes: has its own separate fetchWithRetry (linear backoff, 500ms*attempt) — inconsistent with metaLeadSync.ts's graphGet which has NO retry

modules.name: onboarding.scraper
notes: see scraping_pipeline.pipeline_a for full detail

modules.name: onboarding.analysis
notes: legacy/simpler sibling to marketResearch.ts's deep-research pipeline — no live web search, single runStructured call per step

modules.name: onboarding.marketResearch
notes: see ai_pipeline entries and scraping_pipeline.pipeline_c for full detail
process-local 1hr cache keyed by normalized prompt

modules.name: onboarding.researchSessionService
notes: findCachedSession: 1hr cache window (SESSION_CACHE_WINDOW_MS) — DB-level counterpart to marketResearch.ts's in-process cache

appendResearchBlock does a READ-THEN-WRITE (fetch row, append to blocks array, increment searchCount) — NOT atomic, a potential race if two steps for the same session ran concurrently (unlikely given sequential pipeline design)

modules.name: drafts.draftsService
notes: updateDraft/updateAd previously accepted raw unvalidated req.body (mass-assignment risk) — FIXED this session with zod schemas restricting fields (excluding id/workspaceId)
generateAiRecommendation swallows errors via try/catch — draft still saves without a recommendation on failure

modules.name: strategist.strategistService
notes: 'Chat with Strategist' panel on Media Plan page — already fully functional with real OpenAI, confirmed live-tested this session

modules.name: copilot.copilotService
notes: BUILT this session — the global floating 'AI Copilot' chat was previously 100% hardcoded keyword-matched fake replies with no API call at all; now grounds every answer in real per-campaign performance data (impressions/clicks/conversions/CTR/ROAS) and explicitly refuses to claim it executed any action it can't actually perform

modules.name: support.supportTicketService
notes: built this session to replace a fake Help Center / Help Widget mock form

modules.name: admin.rbacService
notes: built this session; one row per workspace, id=workspaceId

modules.name: admin.developerPortalService
notes: built this session, replacing a hardcoded placeholder API key literal that was baked into the frontend bundle — keys are now server-generated via crypto.randomBytes

modules.name: billing.paymentMethodService
notes: built this session; deliberately NEVER stores a raw card number or CVC, only masked brand/last4/expiry, after server-side Luhn+expiry validation

modules.name: billing.billingEngine
notes: computes FLAT_MONTHLY_FEE_CENTS + 12% of ad spend purely as an internal Prisma-persisted record — NO outbound call to any payment API anywhere in the codebase

modules.name: automation.automationRuleService
notes: built this session, replacing a local-state-only mock CRUD

modules.name: optimization.optimizationEngine
notes: ZERO LLM involvement — pure heuristic/bandit budget-reallocation engine, despite feeding an 'AI Insights' feed downstream

modules.name: optimization.optimizationGoalService
notes: built this session; one row per workspace, id=workspaceId

modules.name: workspace (auth-service side)
notes: getMemberById/getMembership added this session to support real membership-check enforcement in auth-service (previously these routes had NO auth at all — full unauthenticated IDOR, fixed and re-verified live)

modules.name: auth (auth-service side)
notes: updateUser added this session for the 'Your Profile' save feature
passwords hashed with per-user salt + SHA-256 + a static app-secret pepper (crypto.ts's hashPassword)

gateway_apps_api.auth: none at HTTP layer, hub.verify_token check

gateway_apps_api.note: zod-restricted this session, was raw-body mass-assignment before

database.design_pattern: MOST models store their payload as an opaque 'data Json' column rather than normalized columns — deliberate SQLite-to-Postgres driver-swap decision documented in the schema file's own header comment, not an oversight.
database.soft_references_note: every other cross-model reference (businessId, campaignId, workspaceId on nearly every model, adId, adSetId) is a plain indexed string column with NO database-level referential integrity — 100% application-enforced.

models.name: Integration
models.note: no @@unique on [workspaceId,platform] despite app assuming one row per platform per workspace

fields.data: Json (masked brand/last4/expiry ONLY — never a card number or CVC)

ai_pipeline.client_gate: openai export is null unless OPENAI_API_KEY is set; every call site branches on this for a deterministic fallback

helpers.name: runStructured<T>
helpers.model: gpt-4o (DEFAULT_MODEL, never overridden by any call site)
helpers.mechanism: forced single named tool-call (tool_choice: {type:'function', function:{name}}), returns parsed JSON args or null

helpers.name: runText
helpers.mechanism: plain chat completion, returns message.content or null

helpers.name: runWebSearch
helpers.mechanism: single-message call with web_search_options:{}, extracts url_citation annotations into Citation[], searchesUsed=1 if any citation returned else 0

pipelines.name: deep_research_5_blocks
pipelines.mode: runWebSearch -> runStructured for 4 of 5 blocks; mineAudiencePersonas uses runStructured ONLY, deliberately never live search (cost control, training-data taxonomy is 'good enough')
pipelines.search_budget: MAX_SEARCHES_PER_SESSION=16 per session, enforced by caller (researchSessionWorker)
fallback_chain: static hardcoded text (if sidecar output fails blockUsable() heuristic)

pipelines.name: strategist_chat
pipelines.system_prompt_summary: 'You are the Polluxa Strategist, an expert media-buying assistant embedded in the Media Plan section... help the user generate, revise, and evaluate their media plan'
pipelines.status: already fully functional, verified live this session

pipelines.name: copilot_chat
pipelines.system_prompt_summary: 'You are the CRM Ads Copilot... analyze spend/ROAS/CTR, flag underperforming campaigns, draft ad copy... You cannot directly execute changes... dont claim youve submitted or prepared anything'
pipelines.grounding_data: business profile, account-wide analytics summary, per-campaign snapshots (impressions/clicks/conversions/CTR/ROAS via normalizePerformance)
pipelines.status: BUILT this session — previously 100% hardcoded keyword-matched fake replies with zero API calls

pipelines.name: draft_ai_recommendation
pipelines.system_prompt_summary: 'You are an ad strategist reviewing a draft campaign before launch. In 2-3 sentences, give one concrete, specific recommendation'
pipelines.on_failure: swallowed via try/catch, draft still saves without a recommendation

pipelines.name: ai_insights
pipelines.input: flattened normalizePerformance data across all business campaigns

pipelines.name: creative_brief
pipelines.output: {headline, body, callToAction, imagePrompt} — imagePrompt is the load-bearing bridge into image generation

pipelines.name: creative_variations
pipelines.mode: runStructured, forces exactly 3 items each with a distinct persuasion angle

pipelines.name: product_import_llm_normalize
pipelines.purpose: fills gaps (price/category/features) a deterministic JSON-LD parse left ambiguous

not_openai_but_ai_adjacent.name: image_generation
not_openai_but_ai_adjacent.provider: OpenAI, but raw fetch to https://api.openai.com/v1/images/generations, model gpt-image-1 — bypasses the SDK helpers entirely, separate REST surface, no retry

not_openai_but_ai_adjacent.name: video_generation
not_openai_but_ai_adjacent.provider: Runway (NOT OpenAI), model gen4_turbo, image-to-video only, credential RUNWAY_API_KEY

not_openai_but_ai_adjacent.name: campaign_optimizer
not_openai_but_ai_adjacent.provider: NONE — pure heuristic/bandit budget-reallocation logic, zero LLM calls despite feeding an 'AI Insights' feed downstream

pipeline_a_onboarding_crawler.technology: plain fetch + cheerio, NO real browser
steps: fire parallel best-effort screenshot request to scraper-service /products/scrape (12s timeout, never throws)
fetch entry page (8s AbortController timeout, custom UA), cheerio.load, strip script/style/noscript/svg/nav/footer
extract title/description/headings/body text
extract images: og:image preferred, then /data-src, absolute-resolved, SVGs excluded, capped 8
page discovery: sitemap.xml -> robots.txt Sitemap: directive -> same-origin homepage links (in that fallback order); sitemap index files followed up to 3 children
score and crawl top 14 additional pages (15 total incl. entry), 8s per-fetch timeout, 45s TOTAL wall-clock crawl budget (breaks early, keeps partial results)
concatenate into one excerpt, cap ~6000 chars x 15 pages
await screenshot promise, return ScrapedSite{url,title,description,excerpt,images,crawledPages,pagesDiscovered,screenshot}
known_gaps: robots.txt Disallow rules NOT honored (only used to locate sitemap)
no cross-request rate limiting across concurrent onboarding submissions
no retry on individual page fetch failure (by design — skip and continue)

pipeline_b_product_import.technology: Playwright, real headless chromium, shared browser instance + per-request isolated BrowserContext

stages.name: Scrape Worker
stages.detail: page.goto(waitUntil:'domcontentloaded', 20s nav timeout); page.waitForLoadState('networkidle', 8s) is BEST-EFFORT/non-fatal; extracts via page.evaluate() with hand-written plain-string scripts (esbuild __name helper breaks in isolated page context otherwise); captures above-the-fold JPEG screenshot (quality 60), accessibility-tree ariaSnapshot (cap 20k chars), raw HTML (cap 50k chars), body text (cap 8k chars)

stages.name: Image Worker
stages.detail: buckets by source tag (og/logo-home/logo-name/logo-position/img), validates reachability via HEAD requests (5s timeout, concurrency 4), filters sprite/icon/favicon/pixel/spinner/placeholder noise, returns {productImages(max8), heroImages(max2), logoCandidate}

stages.name: Product Parser
stages.detail: DETERMINISTIC, no LLM — reads schema.org JSON-LD Product node (incl @graph-nested), falls back to page metadata/validated images if no JSON-LD

stages.name: LLM Normalizer
stages.detail: OpenAI gpt-4o via runStructured, fills gaps the deterministic draft left ambiguous; falls back to passthrough if no OPENAI_API_KEY

stages.name: Vector Index
stages.detail: hash-based (non-semantic) embedding, queries in-process vectorStore BEFORE upserting so it never matches itself

pipeline_b_product_import.gateway_proxy: POST /api/products/scrape (stage 1 only) and /api/products/import (all 7 stages) via proxyTo(SCRAPER_SERVICE_URL) — NO timeout/retry on the proxy hop itself

pipeline_c_deep_research.reuses: Pipeline A (scrapeUrl) as its sole data-gathering step — all of Pipeline A's gaps flow upstream into this pipeline too

scraping_pipeline.scrapegraphai_confirmation: INITIALLY ASSUMED ABSENT — VERIFIED PRESENT via direct grep. apps/scraper-service/python/research_server.py imports scrapegraphai.graphs.SmartScraperGraph, wraps as FastAPI on port 5055, used exclusively as marketResearch.ts's no-OPENAI_API_KEY fallback.

integrations.name: meta_graph_api
integrations.api_version: v22.0 (hardcoded, duplicated identically across 4 files — no shared constant module)
integrations.auth: OAuth authorization-code flow -> short-lived token -> long-lived token exchange (~60 days); OR manual paste (Graph API Explorer) for testing
integrations.token_storage: AES-256-GCM encrypted (infra/crypto.ts), stored in Integration.settings.accessTokenEncrypted + tokenExpiresAt
integrations.token_refresh: NONE — long-lived tokens never refreshed/rotated once issued, no expiry-driven re-auth trigger
integrations.mock_fallback: if META_APP_ID/META_APP_SECRET unset, OAuth start/callback silently completes a fully-populated MOCK connection instead of hitting Facebook
integrations.multi_account_picker: NO — only first ad account + first Page + linked Instagram account used, explicitly deferred as follow-up
integrations.campaign_hierarchy: real Campaign->AdSet->Creative->Ad graph via /act_{id}/campaigns, /act_{id}/adsets, /act_{id}/advideos|adimages, /act_{id}/adcreatives, /act_{id}/ads — everything lands PAUSED
integrations.currency_handling: toMetaMinorUnits()/META_MINOR_UNIT_DIVISORS correctly handles zero-decimal (JPY,KRW,VND: divisor 1) and 3-decimal (KWD,BHD,OMR,JOD: divisor 1000) currencies for the hierarchy path; KNOWN BUG: the flat setBudget() method hardcodes 'USD' since its input type doesn't carry currency
integrations.video_upload: waitForVideoProcessing() polls /{videoId}?fields=status every 3s up to 40 attempts (~2min), throws on error/timeout — avoids referencing an unprocessed video
integrations.retry: fetchWithRetry (3 attempts, exponential backoff 500ms*2^i) in metaAdapter.ts ONLY — metaOAuth.ts and metaTargetingMapper.ts use plain fetch, no retry
integrations.rate_limit_handling: NONE — no inspection of X-Business-Use-Case-Usage or 429 status anywhere
integrations.webhook: GET /leadgen (hub.challenge verification handshake), POST /leadgen (HMAC-SHA256 X-Hub-Signature-256 over raw body, timingSafeEqual, 401 if secret unset/mismatched); valid webhooks enqueue one lead-ingestion job per lead (async, correctly acks 200 fast)
integrations.crm_proxy: /api/crm/workspaces/:id/ads-data/* converts internal spend-cents metrics into cost_micros (spendCents*10000) so the CRM's Google-Ads-shaped UI works for Meta data unmodified

integrations.name: google_ads_api
integrations.auth: OAuth authorization-code + refresh-token flow, access_type=offline&prompt=consent (forces refresh token on every consent grant), scope https://www.googleapis.com/auth/adwords
integrations.requires: OAuth client id+secret AND a separate developer token (hasLiveGoogleAppCredentials checks all 3 — stricter than Meta's 2-value check)
integrations.token_refresh: YES — getGoogleAdsCredentials() proactively refreshes within a 2-minute skew window (REFRESH_SKEW_MS); the ONLY integration in the app with real token-lifecycle handling
integrations.token_storage: AES-256-GCM encrypted, access+refresh tokens stored separately
integrations.campaign_hierarchy: campaignBudgets:mutate -> campaigns:mutate (sets containsEuPoliticalAdvertising: DOES_NOT_CONTAIN_EU_POLITICAL_ADVERTISING) -> campaignCriteria:mutate (geo/language) -> optional campaignConversionGoals:mutate (best-effort) -> adGroups:mutate -> adGroupCriteria:mutate (age/gender/keywords, best-effort — comment notes age/gender only valid for Display/Video not the Search campaigns this adapter creates) -> adGroupAds:mutate
integrations.orphan_cleanup: cleanupOrphanedBudget() removes a just-created campaignBudget if a later step throws (Google Ads has no cross-resource transaction)
integrations.rsa_minimum: buildResponsiveSearchAdAssets() synthesizes 3 headlines/2 descriptions from the single headline/body/CTA this app generates (Google requires >=3/>=2), truncated to 30/90 chars with ellipsis
integrations.retry: fetchWithRetry (3x exponential) in googleAdapter.ts; SEPARATE inconsistent linear-backoff copy in googleLeadSyncService.ts; OAuth/geo-target calls use plain fetch, no retry
integrations.insights_estimation: Search campaigns have no native reach metric — estimated as impressions*0.65 even on the LIVE path, not just mock
integrations.lead_sync: GAQL against asset(type=LEAD_FORM) and lead_form_submission_data tables, incremental via sinceIso filter, default LAST_30_DAYS
integrations.geo_targeting: geoTargetConstants:suggest live call, falls back to STATIC_GEO_TARGET_IDS (hardcoded table of 13 countries) if call fails/unavailable

integrations.name: tiktok_ads
integrations.auth: static env-var credentials only (TIKTOK_ACCESS_TOKEN, TIKTOK_ADVERTISER_ID) — NO OAuth flow, NO per-workspace connection, unlike Meta/Google
integrations.api: TikTok Business API v1.3, header Access-Token
integrations.hierarchy_support: flat AdAdapter interface ONLY — no HierarchyCapableAdapter (no campaign/ad-group/creative object graph)
integrations.test_authenticity_flag: despite the filename tiktokAdapter.live.test.ts, it mocks global.fetch entirely — NO evidence this adapter has ever been exercised against the real TikTok API

integrations.name: shopify_catalog
integrations.pagination: NONE — hard single page of 50 items, silent truncation of larger catalogs
integrations.on_failure: falls back to hardcoded MOCK_CATALOGS (productCatalogService.ts), error swallowed not surfaced

integrations.name: openai_chat
integrations.auth: OPENAI_API_KEY env var, SDK picks it up implicitly

integrations.name: openai_images
integrations.mechanism: raw fetch to https://api.openai.com/v1/images/generations, size 1024x1024, returns base64 b64_json decoded to PNG buffer
integrations.bypasses: the SDK client and openaiClient.ts helpers entirely — a separate, uncoordinated REST call

integrations.name: runway_video
integrations.flow: POST /image_to_video (promptImage, promptText, ratio:1280:720, duration:5) -> task id -> poll GET /tasks/{id} every 4s up to 45 attempts (~3min) -> download output video URL directly
integrations.retry: NONE on any of its 3 fetch calls (create/poll/download)

integrations.name: scrapegraphai_sidecar
integrations.type: internal/first-party, not a true third-party vendor
integrations.purpose: no-OPENAI_API_KEY fallback for the deep research pipeline only
integrations.caching: 1hr positive cache, 60s negative cache on failure

not_present_third_party_services: No payment processor (Stripe/Braintree/etc.) — billing is an internally-computed invoice record only
No email/SMS vendor (SendGrid/Twilio/Postmark) — notifications are in-app Postgres rows only
No analytics vendor (Segment/Mixpanel/Amplitude) — self-hosted analyticsStore/analyticsService only
No real vector DB vendor — infra/vectorStore.ts is an explicit in-memory placeholder with a hash-based non-semantic embedding
No object storage vendor (S3/GCS/R2) — infra/objectStorage.ts is LocalFileObjectStorage writing to local disk

dependencies.type: direct TypeScript relative import (cross-package, same process boundary conceptually but not deployable independently)
in-process — a layering leak, reaches past the orchestrator's public API

request_flow.name: in_process_fast_path
steps: Browser -> gateway with Authorization: Bearer <jwt>
cors() -> express.json() -> apiRateLimiter (600/60s/IP) -> requireAuth (JWT check, dev-mode bypass if header absent and NODE_ENV!=production)
router.ts: zod savedAudienceSchema.safeParse(req.body)
savedAudienceService.createSavedAudience(workspaceId, data) -> Prisma insert
201 response, single round trip, no proxy hop, no queue

request_flow.name: proxied_synchronous_path
steps: Browser -> gateway -> requireAuth
router.ts: campaignProxy -> proxyTo(CAMPAIGN_SERVICE_URL) — raw fetch, NO timeout, NO retry
campaign-service: internalServiceAuth (x-internal-service-key check) -- NO per-user JWT check at this hop
route handler calls campaignOrchestrator.launchCampaign(campaignId, workspaceId) (imported directly, not HTTP)

launchCampaign splits variants by network, builds real Meta/Google object graphs (or mocked IDs if no live credentials), per-variant try/catch marks that variant failed and CONTINUES (no rollback of partially-created ad-platform objects)
on success: eventBus.publish('campaign.launched', ...) — logged only, no other subscriber today
response relayed back through proxyTo() to browser verbatim

request_flow.name: proxied_async_path
steps: Browser -> gateway -> requireAuth -> zod validate
generationJobService.createGenerationJob (status='queued')
creativeGenerationQueue.add() (BullMQ/Redis)
202 Accepted returned IMMEDIATELY — browser does not wait for generation to finish
SEPARATELY, creativeGenerationWorker (its own OS process) picks up the job whenever Redis delivers it
brief generation (OpenAI) -> image generation (OpenAI gpt-image-1 raw fetch) -> optional video (Runway) -> asset upload (local disk) -> createCreative -> job marked done
browser polls GET /generation-jobs/:id separately to observe completion — NO websocket/SSE push exists anywhere in the app

request_flow.name: webhook_driven_async_path
steps: Meta POSTs to /api/webhooks/meta/leadgen
raw body captured pre-JSON-parse (express.json verify callback) specifically so HMAC can be computed over exact bytes
isValidSignature() verifies X-Hub-Signature-256 via HMAC-SHA256 + timingSafeEqual against META_APP_SECRET; 401 if unset/mismatched
resolve workspace by linear-scanning all meta-platform Integration rows for matching pageId (metaLeadSync.resolveWorkspaceIdForMetaPage — O(n) scan, flagged scalability concern)
for each lead in payload: leadIngestionQueue.add('ingest-one', {workspaceId, leadgenId})
res.sendStatus(200) returned to Meta immediately — ack happens before any lead is actually processed

leadIngestionWorker (separate process) later calls ingestMetaLead() -> fetches field_data via Graph API -> leadIngestionService.ingestLead() -> upsert Lead -> (if genuinely new) contactService.upsertContactForLead + crmWebhookService.dispatchCrmWebhook

data_flow.name: research_to_campaign
data_flow.flow: User enters URL -> POST /research-sessions (202, queued) -> researchSessionWorker: scrapeUrl (Pipeline A) -> 5 sequential AI blocks (product/audience/competitor+budget/market/personas), each written incrementally to ResearchSession.blocks so the frontend's polling GET shows partial progress -> session marked done -> frontend calls POST /businesses/:id/strategies/from-research (NO new AI call, reuses research JSON) -> strategyEngine.generateCampaignSuggestions produces 12 suggestions (6 Meta + 6 Google) -> POST /campaigns/from-suggestions proxied to campaign-service -> campaignOrchestrator.buildCampaignFromSuggestions creates 1 CampaignVariant per suggestion (1:1, no cross-product) -> Campaign persisted, status='draft' -> rendered in Campaign Builder for review/launch

data_flow.name: campaign_launch_to_ad_platform
data_flow.flow: POST /campaigns/:id/launch -> campaignOrchestrator.launchCampaign -> splits CampaignVariant[] by network -> Meta variants grouped by audienceName into Ad Sets (metaTargetingMapper.resolveAudienceTargetingForWorkspace bridges the free-text audienceName to a real SavedAudience by case-insensitive name match, falling back to broad-default targeting on no match) -> real Campaign->AdSet->Creative->Ad Graph API calls, everything created PAUSED -> Google variants similarly grouped into Ad Groups with Responsive Search Ads synthesized from headline/body/CTA -> campaign.networks recomputed from ACTUAL post-launch variant outcomes (not the strategy-time frozen value) -> campaign.status derived from variant statuses (active > paused > failed) -> 'campaign.launched' event published (logged only)

data_flow.name: metrics_to_insights
data_flow.flow: metricsIngestionWorker (15-min repeatable tick) -> listActiveCampaigns() (all-workspace Postgres JSON-path scan) -> per campaign SEQUENTIALLY: ingestCampaignMetrics (pulls fresh data from adapters[network].fetchInsights, writes to analyticsStore) -> runOptimizationPass (pure heuristic, NO LLM — decides pause/increase_budget/decrease_budget/hold per variant) -> recordOptimizationInsights (persists non-'hold' decisions as permanent 'optimizer'-source Insight rows, distinct from the wholesale-replaced 'ai_generated' rows from insightService.generateInsights) -> surfaced in the AI Insights dashboard feed

data_flow.name: lead_to_crm
data_flow.flow: Meta/Google lead arrives (webhook push for Meta, GAQL poll for Google) -> leadIngestionService.ingestLead: upsert Lead on (workspaceId,platform,externalId) compound unique -> IF genuinely new (checked before upsert): contactService.upsertContactForLead (dedupe by email first, phone fallback, merges into Contact without clobbering existing non-null fields) AND crmWebhookService.dispatchCrmWebhook (enqueues 'lead.created', HMAC-signed if a workspace secret is configured) -> crmWebhookWorker delivers with 5s timeout, 5-attempt custom backoff (30s/2m/10m/30m/2h), permanently-failed jobs sit in Redis 30 days as the de facto DLQ

missing_components: No payment processor (Stripe/etc.) — billing is an internally-computed invoice record only, no outbound charge capability
No email/SMS delivery (SendGrid/Twilio/Postmark) — notifications are in-app-only Postgres rows
No real vector database — infra/vectorStore.ts is an explicit in-memory placeholder with a hash-based non-semantic embedding
No real object storage (S3/GCS/R2) — infra/objectStorage.ts writes to local disk, will not survive horizontal scaling or ephemeral containers
No containerization for the apps themselves — docker-compose.yml provisions Postgres+Redis only; no Dockerfile exists for apps/api, apps/web, or the 3 sibling services

No observability stack — no APM/distributed tracing, no correlated request IDs across the gateway->proxied-service hop, no Bull Board/Arena for queue visibility, no alerting on queue depth/failed-job counts
No API versioning — /api/* has no /v1/ prefix or equivalent
No rate-limit-aware handling of any external API (no 429/Retry-After inspection anywhere)
No token rotation/refresh for Meta (Google has it, Meta doesn't)
No secrets manager — env vars with hardcoded dev-fallback defaults (e.g. JWT_SECRET='dev-secret-change-me') instead of Vault/AWS Secrets Manager
auth-service and campaign-service cannot be deployed independently of apps/api's source tree — a structural coupling, not a feature gap, but blocks true independent service deployment/scaling
No multi-account picker for Meta/Google connect flows — both take only the first ad account/Page/customer found
No TikTok OAuth / per-workspace connection — single global env-var credential unlike Meta/Google's real multi-tenant OAuth
No pagination on Shopify/WooCommerce catalog fetch — hard-capped at 50 items, silently truncating larger catalogs
No websocket/SSE push for async job status — frontend polls GET endpoints on a timer for generation jobs and research sessions
No shared retry/timeout wrapper for external HTTP calls — retry logic exists in only 3 of 9 external integrations, inconsistently even there
No compound unique DB constraint on Integration(workspaceId, platform) despite the app assuming one row per platform per workspace
No CI beyond typecheck+build (no test execution gate visible in ci.yml, no deployment pipeline)

scalability_notes.reason: single Postgres + single Redis handle this trivially; in-memory vector store/event bus are non-issues at this volume; O(n) Meta-webhook workspace lookup is negligible
notificationService.markAllRead's per-row loop and analyticsService's week/month re-derivation start to show as measurable (not critical) latency; still single-instance-safe

the in-memory vector store and in-process event bus become CORRECTNESS bugs (not just perf) the moment the gateway runs more than one replica — each replica has its own independent, empty vector index/event subscribers, so data written on replica A is invisible on replica B. metricsIngestionWorker's single-concurrency sequential fan-out risks ticks overlapping/backing up. proxyTo()'s no-timeout gap becomes a real cascading-failure risk under load. REQUIRES: horizontal-scale-safe vector store (real vector DB) and event bus (Redis pub/sub minimum, Kafka per the existing architecture-roadmap.md), plus parallelizing the metrics-ingestion fan-out

scalability_notes.verdict: not without executing the Phase 3-4 roadmap items
scalability_notes.reason: needs everything in the 10000-tier fix, PLUS: the Json-blob-per-model schema stops being adequate for analytics/reporting query patterns at this scale (currently JS-side filtering of Postgres JSON blobs); local-disk object storage doesn't survive multi-instance deployment at all; no CDN/caching layer for frontend or generated creative assets; no confirmed Prisma connection-pooling strategy for many gateway/worker replicas
